title: Incorrect Trie Node Initialization Due to Missing Child Node Deletion

statement.markdown: Write a function to insert a word into a trie. Note that the function should handle cases where the word has already been inserted and return True if the word has been successfully inserted, otherwise return False. The trie is a null-terminated binary tree where each node has a character and a boolean value to indicate whether a word ends at this node, and pointers to the child nodes.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be a string representing the word to be inserted and a pointer to the root node of the trie.
statement.outputFormat: Return a boolean indicating whether the word has been successfully inserted.
statement.notes: This is a basic implementation of a trie insertion function.

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
